# Diagram: research/orchestrator/tasks/etl/extract_public_entityplannedtripleg_spark.py


> Auto-generated by Obscura crawlers

## Diagram 1

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    Start([Start]) --> ParseArgs[Parse command line arguments<br/>DB_HOSTNAME, DB_USERNAME,<br/>DB_PASSWORD, DATABASE, table]
    ParseArgs --> InitSpark[Initialize SparkContext<br/>master=yarn]
    InitSpark --> ConfigSpark[Configure Spark<br/>network timeout, executor idle,<br/>decimal settings, parquet settings]
    ConfigSpark --> CreateSQL[Create SQLContext]
    CreateSQL --> QueryMaxId[Query max ID from<br/>public.entity_planned_trip_leg]
    QueryMaxId --> CheckMaxId{Max ID<br/>retrieved?}
    CheckMaxId -->|Success| UseMaxId[Use retrieved max_id]
    CheckMaxId -->|Exception| UseDefault[Use default max_id=6000000000]
    UseMaxId --> ReadTable[Read table from PostgreSQL<br/>with partitioning<br/>1000 partitions]
    UseDefault --> ReadTable
    ReadTable --> WriteParquet[Write to S3 parquet<br/>mode=overwrite]
    WriteParquet --> GetLatestPath[Call get_latest_path<br/>to remove version folder]
    GetLatestPath --> ReadBack[Read parquet from<br/>versioned S3 path]
    ReadBack --> WriteLatest[Write to latest S3 path<br/>mode=overwrite]
    WriteLatest --> End([End])
```

## Diagram 2

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant Main
    participant SparkContext
    participant SQLContext
    participant PostgreSQL
    participant S3
    
    Main->>Main: Parse sys.argv
    Main->>SparkContext: Initialize with YARN
    Main->>SparkContext: Set configuration options
    Main->>SQLContext: Create SQLContext
    Main->>SQLContext: Read JDBC (max ID query)
    SQLContext->>PostgreSQL: SELECT max(id)
    PostgreSQL-->>SQLContext: Return max_id
    SQLContext-->>Main: max_id_df
    Main->>Main: Extract max_id or use default
    Main->>SQLContext: Read JDBC (full table)
    SQLContext->>PostgreSQL: SELECT * with partitioning
    PostgreSQL-->>SQLContext: Return DataFrame
    SQLContext-->>Main: df
    Main->>S3: Write parquet (versioned path)
    Main->>Main: get_latest_path()
    Main->>SQLContext: Read parquet (versioned)
    SQLContext->>S3: Read parquet data
    S3-->>SQLContext: Return DataFrame
    SQLContext-->>Main: df
    Main->>S3: Write parquet (latest path)
```

## Diagram 3

```mermaid
graph LR
    A[get_latest_path] --> B[Split path by '/']
    B --> C[Remove second-to-last element<br/>version folder]
    C --> D[Join parts back with '/']
    D --> E[Return deduplicated path]
```
